---
title: "Architecture Reference"
subtitle: "How the Codex Version Works"
---

# System Overview

The Codex-native Clo-Author has five active layers:

| Layer | Location | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| Root constitution | `AGENTS.md` | Always-loaded project rules |
| Plugin manifest | `plugins/clo-author/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` | Registers the repo-local plugin |
| Skills | `plugins/clo-author/skills/` | Natural-language workflows |
| References | `plugins/clo-author/references/` | Domain, journal, workflow, and role guidance |
| File-backed memory | `MEMORY.md`, `quality_reports/` | Durable context across sessions |

The legacy `.claude/` tree is no longer part of the active runtime. It remains only as source material during the migration.

# Worker-Critic Model

The architecture still uses specialized roles, but Codex handles them through skills and optional sub-agent delegation.

| Role Type | Responsibility | File Mutation |
|-----------|----------------|---------------|
| Worker | Produces artifacts | May edit |
| Critic | Reviews and scores | Read-only |
| Referee | Blind peer-review report | Read-only |
| Infrastructure | Routing, verification, synthesis | Read-only by default |

The active role catalog is in `plugins/clo-author/references/agent-catalog.md`.

# Execution Loop

1. Read `AGENTS.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and the relevant references.
2. Create a spec if the request is ambiguous.
3. Save a plan to `quality_reports/plans/`.
4. Execute with the appropriate worker role.
5. Review with the paired critic.
6. Verify outputs mechanically.
7. Save the outcome and next step.

# Delegation Policy

Codex sub-agents are useful, but only when the work is truly parallelizable.

- Use sub-agents for bounded subtasks with disjoint ownership.
- Keep critics read-only.
- Run blind referees independently.
- Prefer 2-4 concurrent roles.
- Fall back to sequential execution when the subtasks are tightly coupled or when sub-agents are unavailable.

# Quality Gates

| Gate | Threshold |
|------|-----------|
| Commit | 80 |
| PR | 90 |
| Submission | 95 overall and every scored component >= 80 |
| Talks | Advisory only |

The historical scoring rubric remains in `scripts/quality_score.py` and the migrated reference material under `plugins/clo-author/references/legacy-rules/`.

# Persistence Model

Codex does not depend on hidden session memory in this architecture.

| Artifact | Path | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| Durable lessons | `MEMORY.md` | Prevent repeated mistakes |
| Requirements specs | `quality_reports/specs/` | Clarify ambiguous tasks |
| Plans | `quality_reports/plans/` | Preserve task strategy |
| Session logs | `quality_reports/session_logs/` | Preserve decision history |
| Reviews and memos | `quality_reports/` | Store outputs from skills |

# Hook Replacement Strategy

The Claude version used automatic hooks. The Codex version keeps the behavior, but makes it explicit:

- file protection becomes instruction-level policy
- compaction recovery becomes a saved-plan routine
- post-merge learning becomes a `tools` workflow

See `plugins/clo-author/references/hook-replacements.md`.
